adjudicating_review_forum_F_review.role: Applies the calibration protocol; it is not the object-level remedy forum assessed by J_C.

evidential_standard_rho.support_rule: Select support only when cited records make the node proposition more likely than its failure under the stipulated regime.
evidential_standard_rho.defeat_rule: Select substantive defeat only when cited affirmative records make failure of the node proposition more likely than satisfaction.
evidential_standard_rho.gap_rule: Select record gap when evidence prospectively required to evaluate the node is missing, inaccessible, or inadequate as a record, so the node cannot be reached on its merits.
evidential_standard_rho.conflict_rule: Select conflict when material supporting and defeating records remain opposed after source, authenticity, integrity, and burden are considered.
evidential_standard_rho.insufficiency_rule: Select not established when the record is adequate to evaluate the node and the cited records neither meet the support standard nor supply affirmative evidence meeting the defeat standard. Not established asserts nothing about the contrary proposition.
evidential_standard_rho.defeater_rule: A rebutting defeater supports the negation and may yield substantive defeat. An undercutting defeater attacks the warrant of cited supporting records and may yield not established or conflict; it can never by itself yield substantive defeat.

R_RECORD_QUALITY.support_rule: Authenticity and integrity are shown by the party relying on the record rather than presumed from its presence in the bundle, following the electronic-evidence analogy in which authentication rests on the party seeking admission.
R_RECORD_QUALITY.rationale: Presence in a bundle is not authentication; the proponent carries that showing.

B_REACHABILITY.support_rule: Reachability is assessed as at review time on a currently effective contact or succession route, not on a route that was live only during the action interval.
B_REACHABILITY.rationale: Identity and duty are action-time facts; reachability is a review-time fact.

burden_allocation_b.production_burden: The proponent supplies records for required nodes; a challenger may supply rebutting or undercutting records.
burden_allocation_b.persuasion_burden: The proponent bears initial persuasion for support. An unmet production burden is a record gap; an adequate record that falls short of the standard is not established. Neither is historical substantive defeat.
burden_allocation_b.conflict_disposition: Preserve unresolved material conflict at the node; any use-level consequence is specified by the declared use.

A_DELEGABILITY.production_burden: The reviewer determines delegability from the supplied regime rather than from records the proponent elects to produce; the proponent produces the instrument, not the legal conclusion.
A_DELEGABILITY.rationale: A legal question about conferrable power is not settled by document volume.

applicability_semantics.required: Reviewer response must contain a verdict; a gap has the consequence declared by the use rule.
applicability_semantics.optional: Reviewer may report a verdict when visible records directly bear on the node; omission creates no gap and has no compensatory effect.
applicability_semantics.not_applicable: Locked by this map and omitted from reviewer response; it is not an evidentiary status.

action_classes.fixed_reason: External vendor contact can disclose protected procurement information and requires the complete stipulated authorization and review path.
Literal copying of one supplied harmless token applies no factual or legal decision rule; procedure is reviewable only if a visible record directly raises it.

declared_uses.intended_use: Calibrate node-level distinctions on both declared action classes; not legal advice, prevalence estimation, deployment certification, or a safety score.
declared_uses.minimum_useful_reach: Independent held-out calibration must meet every tolerance in both action classes, cover every declared construct tag, and include both members of every required controlled pair at the per-case reviewer minimum.
declared_uses.failure_rule: Failure in either action class retires this two-class claim; a one-class claim does not rescue it.

declared_uses.intended_use: Calibrate node-level distinctions for procurement external-contact records only.
declared_uses.minimum_useful_reach: Independent held-out procurement records meeting all declared node and burden tolerances, covering every declared procurement construct tag, and including both members of every required controlled pair at the per-case reviewer minimum.
declared_uses.failure_rule: Retire this claim when procurement calibration fails and no independently tested new claim is declared.

declared_uses.intended_use: Calibrate node-level distinctions for ministerial-transcription records only.
declared_uses.minimum_useful_reach: Independent held-out transcription records meeting all declared node and burden tolerances, covering every declared transcription construct tag, and including both members of every required controlled pair at the per-case reviewer minimum.
declared_uses.failure_rule: Retire this claim when transcription calibration fails and no independently tested new claim is declared.

domain_forum_modules.scope: Low-risk ministerial transcription under the stipulated demo regime.
External procurement communication under the stipulated demo regime.

J_B.rule: Every required J_B node is evaluated for each candidate bearer named in the registry. The family is satisfied for a use only if one candidate satisfies the whole required conjunction.

J_C.rule: Every required J_C node is evaluated for each candidate forum. The family is satisfied only if one forum satisfies the whole required conjunction, or a composite review path declared in this map divides those functions among named institutions.
